_description: Configuration for cv_to_ns_commands.py (Cisco Cyber Vision CSV -> Network Sketcher). Every key is optional; omit one to use its code default. Only 'value' is read by the script; 'description' and 'sample' are documentation only. Zone classification is intentionally minimal-automatic: group_zone_override is the authoritative place to pin site-specific group names to a Purdue zone.

version.description: Version of this configuration file schema.

exclude_noise_groups.description: If true, components in noise_groups (broadcast/multicast/etc.) and any component without a routable IPv4 are dropped from the diagram and listed in out_of_scope.csv. Set false to draw everything.

noise_groups.description: Cyber Vision group names treated as non-asset noise and excluded when exclude_noise_groups is true. The empty string '' matches ungrouped components.

synthesize_infrastructure.description: Cyber Vision does not export physical cabling. When true, the tool synthesises a CPwE-style logical backbone (ENT-Core <-> IDMZ-FW <-> IND-Core, with one Industrial-Ethernet access switch per Cell/Area zone) so that the diagram has L1/L2/L3 structure. Set false to emit endpoints with no switches/links.

group_zone_override.description: Authoritative Group -> Purdue zone mapping. Key = exact CV group name; value = one of ENTERPRISE | IDMZ | INDUSTRIAL | CELL. Overrides keyword/device-type inference. Use this to correct any group the auto-classifier (see gen_zone_assignment.csv) placed wrongly.

idmz_straddler_detection.description: If true, any asset whose activities reach BOTH an Enterprise-side zone and an Industrial-side zone is treated as an IT/OT bridge and relocated into the IDMZ. Such assets hang under a synthesised DMZ L2 switch (IDMZ-SW) placed to the LEFT of IDMZ-FW; the DMZ gateway SVI sits on the firewall. Requires synthesize_infrastructure=true to draw the switch.

idmz_enterprise_zones.description: Zones counted as the 'enterprise side' for straddler detection. A straddler must talk to at least one asset in one of these zones.

idmz_industrial_zones.description: Zones counted as the 'industrial side' for straddler detection. A straddler must also talk to at least one asset in one of these zones. Add 'CELL' to also pull in hosts that bridge enterprise and the plant-floor cell/area zones (broader IEC 62443 IDMZ scope).

vlan_base.description: First VLAN id; each area (Enterprise / IDMZ / Industrial / each Cell zone) is assigned the next id in sequence.

devices_per_row.description: Max number of endpoint devices placed per row inside an area's device_location grid (controls diagram width).
